mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 9.0 percent signals labor market stabilization, reducing recession probability and driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 55 percent of months where the unemployment rate crosses below the 9.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in industrial production and housing starts indicates broad-based economic recovery, triggering strong positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in industrial production and housing starts fails to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales during a monetary easing cycle confirm consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns as corporate revenue expectations improve.
failure_condition: Retail sales growth yields negative forward returns when the federal funds rate is below 0.5 percent in greater than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Declining total nonfarm payrolls indicates worsening economic conditions, leading to negative equity returns as corporate earnings expectations fall.
failure_condition: Negative payroll changes correlate with positive 5-day returns in greater than 50 percent of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.5 percent, further rate cuts are impossible, causing markets to price in liquidity traps and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of periods where the federal funds rate stays below 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where GDP growth accelerates while retail sales stagnate signals weak consumer transmission, causing negative equity returns due to earnings uncertainty.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth combined with flat retail sales produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines during a post-shock recovery phase signal fragile supply chains, causing negative equity returns as growth expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production changes yield positive 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of recovery-phase observations.
